assertions.text: The Subaru was traveling northbound in the left lane at about 60 km/h.
The car ahead drifted into the Subaru's lane.
The Subaru's front struck the rear of the car ahead (rear-end collision).
The driver of the Subaru braked just before the collision.
There was a single impact between the Subaru and the car ahead.
The car ahead drifted into the Subaru's lane before the impact occurred.
The Subaru braked after the car ahead drifted into its lane and before the impact.
The collision involved the Subaru's front hitting the other vehicle's rear (severity not explicitly stated).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows the Subaru's recorded speed was [first_value 121.0 km/h](T2) at t=[-5.0 s](T1) and [last_value 111.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T1), which is substantially higher than [60 km/h](T1). The event summary lists vehicle as Subaru Legacy but does not provide compass heading to verify 'northbound' or lane position, so direction/lane are [unverifiable](T1).

There is no lane-change or object detection channel in the telemetry. Steering shows a peak_signed_dev of [35.0 deg](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T2), but that alone does not prove another vehicle drifted into the lane; lane intrusion by the other vehicle is not recorded, so the claim is unverifiable (sensor channels lacking).

The EDR event description is 'Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)' in the summary (t=0) and the crash-pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [81.0 km/h](T1) at t=[185.0 ms](T1), with lateral delta-V of [11.0 km/h](T1), consistent with a front impact into another vehicle's rear (front-to-rear).
The brake channel shows a transition 0->1 at [t = -0.5 s](T2) and detect_events flagged a brake_application at [t = -0.5 s](T3), indicating the driver applied the service brake shortly before impact.

The crash data contains a single recorded crash pulse with max longitudinal delta-V magnitude [81.0 km/h](T1) and the EDR summary lists 'Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)', implying one impact was recorded for this vehicle (no multiple impact events in the summary).

Telemetry lacks direct measurements of the other vehicle's trajectory or a lane-intrusion timestamp; steering peak at [30.0 deg](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T2) does not establish that the other vehicle drifted before impact, so timing of the other vehicle's drift is unverifiable.

Brake application occurred at [t = -0.5 s](T2) and the crash impact is t=[0 s](T1), so the Subaru braked after the other-vehicle drift claim (unverifiable) but importantly braked before impact as shown by the brake transition at [t = -0.5 s](T2) preceding the impact at [t = 0 s](T1).

The EDR labels the event 'Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)' (t=0) and the dominant crash-pulse longitudinal delta-V was [81.0 km/h](T1), indicating the Subaru's front impacted the other vehicle's rear; severity magnitude is shown by the [81.0 km/h](T1) delta-V.

result.rationale: Sensor telemetry from the Subaru (V2) records a single frontal/rear crash pulse with dominant longitudinal delta-V of [81.0 km/h](T1), a brake application at [t = -0.5 s](T2) before impact, and no sensors that record the other vehicle's lane change; assertions about speeds and lane movements are partially unverifiable or contradicted where speed is quantitatively different.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
